mechanism_narrative: Monthly retail sales (RSAFS) above its 12-month moving average indicates strengthening consumer spending, a key driver of corporate earnings, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in at least 55% of months when RSAFS is above its 12-month MA, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production increase exceeding 0.5% signals accelerating manufacturing activity, raising expectations for GDP growth and corporate profits, driving positive forward equity returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: recovery when capacity utilization is low
failure_condition: When triggered, 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or hit rate below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate from near zero to above 0.25% signals the start of monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, leading to negative short-term returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When triggered, 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of occurrences, or average return is greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls rise and unemployment falls simultaneously, the labor market is unambiguously strengthening. This boosts consumer confidence and wage growth expectations, driving equity prices higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 0 and UNRATE month-over-month change < 0
failure_condition: When the joint condition holds, 20-day forward returns are not reliably positive: hit rate < 55% or average return not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts (HOUST) rising from depressed levels signals improving conditions in the housing market, a key lead indicator for residential investment and associated consumption, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of HOUST level with 60-day forward returns < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or the correlation is negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus annual CPI inflation) is negative, monetary policy is accommodative, lowering the discount rate for equities and supporting higher valuations over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus annual CPI inflation rate (CPIAUCSL year-over-year change) < 0
regime_hint: periods of accommodative monetary policy with negative real rates
failure_condition: When real rate is negative, 20-day forward returns are positive in less than 55% of instances, or average return is not significantly above zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE above 8%), increases in nonfarm payrolls signal leveraging of slack without immediate wage inflation, allowing the recovery to continue without monetary tightening, leading to positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 8% and PAYEMS increases, 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of cases, or average return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) is below 2.5%, rising inflation reflects strengthening demand without triggering immediate monetary tightening. This supports positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon; the direction reverses in higher inflation regimes.
failure_condition: When CPI YoY is below 2.5% and increasing, 60-day forward returns are negative in more than half of the cases.
